revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: 「'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかんざき の おさ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。 == 経…」
revision.text:
'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかんざき の おさ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 経歴 == {{Main|巨勢氏}} 「巨勢神前氏」は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第二十七の[天智天皇]]2年3月条にしか登場せず、[[巨勢氏]]の中で、[[近江国]][[神崎郡|神前郡]]に居住した支流としか分かっていない。なお、神前郡は、この後の天智天皇4年2月に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]の遺臣、[[鬼室集斯]]らが亡命したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田を給ふ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}} ともある。
訳語については、[[663年]]（天智天皇2年3月）[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島にわたり、前将軍 [[上毛野稚子|上毛野君稚子]]（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）、後将軍[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]（あべのひけた の おみ ひらふ）とともに「中将軍」を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたことしか分かっていない<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]で果たした役割や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国できたのかどうかも不明である<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条、11日条、13日条、24日条、25日条</ref>。
なお、巨勢氏自体は、『書紀』巻第二十九にある[[八色の姓]]制定により、 [[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）に第2位の[[朝臣]]に改姓している<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[庵原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせのかんさき の おさ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:巨勢氏|おさ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかんさき の おさ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 経歴 == {{Main|巨勢氏}} 「巨勢神前氏」は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第二十七の[[天智天皇]]2年3月条にしか登場せず、[[巨勢氏]]の中で、[[近江国]][[神崎郡|神前郡]]に居住した支流としか分かっていない。なお、神前郡は、この後の天智天皇4年2月（[[665年]]）に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]の遺臣、[[鬼室集斯]]らが亡命したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田を給ふ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}} ともある。
訳語については、[[663年]]（天智天皇2年3月）[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島にわたり、前将軍 [[上毛野稚子|上毛野君稚子]]（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）、後将軍[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]（あべのひけた の おみ ひらふ）とともに「中将軍」を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたことしか分かっていない<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]で果たした役割や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国できたのかどうかも不明である<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条、11日条、13日条、24日条、25日条</ref>。
なお、巨勢氏自体は、『書紀』巻第二十九にある[[八色の姓]]制定により、 [[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）に第2位の[[朝臣]]に改姓している<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[庵原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせのかんさき の おさ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:巨勢氏|おさ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

なお、巨勢氏自体は、『書紀』巻第二十九にある[[八色の姓]]制定により、 [[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）に第2位の[[朝臣]]に改姓している<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[庵原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]

revision.comment: 神前郡のリンクを修正。
revision.text:
'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかんさき の おさ、生没年不詳）は[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 経歴 == {{Main|巨勢氏}} 「巨勢神前氏」は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第二十七の[[天智天皇]]2年3月条にしか登場せず、[[巨勢氏]]の中で、[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神前郡]]に居住した支流としか分かっていない。なお、神前郡は、この後の天智天皇4年2月（[[665年]]）に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]の遺臣、[[鬼室集斯]]らが亡命したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田<small>（た）</small>を給<small>（たま）</small>ふ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}} ともある。
訳語に関する記述は、[[663年]]（天智天皇2年3月）[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島にわたり、前将軍 [[上毛野稚子|上毛野君稚子]]（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）、後将軍[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]（あべのひけた の おみ ひらふ）とともに「中将軍」を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたということだけである<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]で果たした業績や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国できたのかどうかも不明である<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条、11日条、13日条、24日条、25日条</ref>。
なお、巨勢氏自体は、『書紀』巻第二十九にある[[八色の姓]]制定により、 [[684年]]（天武天皇13年11月）に第2位の[[朝臣]]に改姓している<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせのかんさき の おさ}} [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:巨勢氏|おさ]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかんさき の おさ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 経歴 == {{Main|巨勢氏}} 「巨勢神前氏」は『[[日本書紀]]』巻第二十七の[[天智天皇]]2年3月条にしか登場せず、[[巨勢氏]]の中で[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神前郡]]に居住した支流としか分かっていない。なお、神前郡は、この後の天智天皇4年2月（[[665年]]）に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]の遺臣、[[鬼室集斯]]らが亡命したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田<small>（た）</small>を給<small>（たま）</small>ふ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}} ともある。
訳語に関する記述は、天智天皇2年（[[663年]]）3月[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島にわたり、前将軍・[[上毛野稚子|上毛野君稚子]]（かみつけの の きみ わかこ）、後将軍[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田臣比羅夫]]（あべのひけた の おみ ひらふ）と共に「中将軍」を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたということだけである<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]で果たした業績や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国できたのかどうかも不明である<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条、11日条、13日条、24日条、25日条</ref>。
なお、巨勢氏自体は、『書紀』巻第二十九にある[[八色の姓]]制定により、天武天皇13年（[[684年]]）11月に第2位の[[朝臣]]に改姓している<ref>『日本書紀』天武天皇13年11月1日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金ユ信|金庾信]]

== 出自 == 巨勢神前氏（巨勢神前臣）は[[巨勢氏]]の支流で[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神前郡]]に居住した一族。なお、神前郡はこの後の天智天皇4年（[[665年]]）2月に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}

== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}

revision.text:
'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかむさき の おさ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 出自 == 巨勢神前氏（巨勢神前臣）は[[巨勢氏]]の支流で[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神前郡]]に居住した一族。なお、神前郡はこの後の天智天皇4年（[[665年]]）2月に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]遺臣の[[鬼室集斯]]らが[[亡命]]したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田<small>（た）</small>を給<small>（たま）</small>ふ。<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}} ともある。
== 経歴 == 天智天皇2年（[[663年]]）3月に訳語は[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島に渡り、前将軍・[[上毛野稚子]]、後将軍・[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田比羅夫]]と共に中将軍を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたという<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]での活動や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国したかどうかは明らかでない<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条,11日条,13日条,24日条,25日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせのかんさき の おさ}} [[Category:巨勢氏|おさ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

revision.text:
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[Asuka period]]. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|ja|神崎郡 (滋賀県)|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "-qq]]" to "-->[[Category:qq deprecated close]]"
revision.text:
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[Asuka period]]. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|ja|神崎郡 (滋賀県)|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:qq deprecated close]]" to ""
revision.text:
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[Asuka period]]. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|ja|神崎郡 (滋賀県)|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信}}

revision.text:
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[Asuka period]]. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ}} title was {{ill|Omi|ja|臣}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|ja|神崎郡 (滋賀県)|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "↵↵↵↵" to " "
revision.text:
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[Asuka period]]. His {{ill|Kabane|ja|カバネ|12=draft}} title was {{ill|Omi|ja|臣|12=draft}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=draft}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|ja|神崎郡 (滋賀県)|lt=Kanzaki District|12=draft}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|ja|近江国|12=draft}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯|12=draft}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い|12=draft}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=draft}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=draft}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津|12=draft}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=draft}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=draft}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=draft}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌|12=draft}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信|12=draft}}

In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Kishitsu no Jushi|ja|鬼室集斯|12=draft}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|ja|新羅|12=draft}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=draft}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|ja|阿倍比羅夫|12=draft}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|ja|白村江の戦い|12=draft}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=draft}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=draft}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=draft}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=draft}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi|ja|朴市秦田来津|12=draft}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=draft}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=draft}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto|ja|鬼室福信|12=draft}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=draft}} * {{ill|Liu Ren Gui|ja|劉仁軌|12=draft}} * {{ill|King Munmu|ja|文武王|12=draft}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|ja|金庾信|12=draft}}

revision.text:
'''巨勢神前 訳語'''（こせのかむさき の おさ、生没年不詳）は、[[飛鳥時代]]の[[豪族]]。[[カバネ|姓]]は[[臣]]。
== 出自 == 巨勢神前氏（巨勢神前臣）は[[巨勢氏]]の支流で[[近江国]][[神崎郡 (滋賀県)|神前郡]]に居住した一族。なお、神前郡はこの後の天智天皇4年（[[665年]]）2月に、 {{quotation|復<small>（また）</small>百済の百姓<small>（たみ）</small>男<small>（をのこ）</small>女<small>（めのこ）</small>四百余人<small>（よほたりあまり）</small>を以て、近江国<small>（あふみのくに）</small>の神前郡<small>（かむさきのこほり）</small>に居<small>（お）</small>く。<ref name="#1">『日本書紀』天智天皇4年2月条</ref>}}
ともあるところで、[[百済]]遺臣の[[鬼室集斯]]らが[[亡命]]したところである。 {{quotation|「是の月（3月）に、神前郡の百済人<small>（くだらびと）</small>に田<small>（た）</small>を給<small>（たま）</small>ふ。<ref name="#1"/>}} ともある。
== 経歴 == 天智天皇2年（[[663年]]）3月に訳語は[[新羅]]討伐の部隊として半島に渡り、前将軍・[[上毛野稚子]]、後将軍・[[阿倍比羅夫|阿倍引田比羅夫]]と共に中将軍を務め、2万7千人の軍団を率いたという<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年3月条</ref>。同年8月の[[白村江の戦い]]での活動や<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年8月27日条、28日条</ref>、生きて帰国したかどうかは明らかでない<ref>『日本書紀』天智天皇2年9月7日条,11日条,13日条,24日条,25日条</ref>。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}}
== 参考文献 == * 『日本書紀』（五）、[[岩波文庫]]、1994年 - 1995年 * 『日本書紀』全現代語訳（下）、[[講談社学術文庫]]、[[宇治谷孟]]：訳、1988年 * 『日本の歴史2 古代国家の成立』、[[直木孝次郎]]：著、[[中央公論社]]、1965年 * 『日本古代氏族事典』【新装版】[[佐伯有清]]：編、[[雄山閣]]、2015年
== 関連項目 == *[[間人大蓋]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[河辺百枝]]- 白村江の戦いで生き延びた将軍の一人。 *[[三輪根麻呂]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。訳語と同じく中将軍の一人。 *[[安曇比羅夫]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[朴市秦田来津]]- 白村江の戦いで戦没した将軍の一人。 *[[狭井檳榔]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[廬原臣]]- 白村江の戦いの将軍の一人。 *[[鬼室福信]] *[[周留城]] *[[劉仁軌]] *[[文武王]] *[[金庾信]]
{{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:こせのかんさき の おさ}} [[Category:巨勢氏|おさ]] [[Category:飛鳥時代の人物]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:天智天皇]] [[Category:古代日朝関係]] [[Category:白村江の戦い]] [[Category:生没年不詳]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|qq=|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|qq=|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|qq=|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|qq=|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|qq=|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|qq=|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|qq=|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|qq=|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|qq=|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|qq=|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.

== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. == Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the [[:en:Asuka period|Asuka period]]. His [[:en:Kabane|Kabane]] title was [[:en:Omi (title)|Omi]].
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in [[:en:Kanzaki District, Shiga|Kanzaki District]], [[:en:Ōmi Province|Ōmi Province]]. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like [[:en:Gwisil Jipsa|Kishitsu no Jushi]], who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against [[:en:Silla|Silla]], serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general [[:en:Abe no Hirafu|Abe no Hirafu]]. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the [[:en:Battle of Baekgang|Battle of Baekgang]] is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * [[:en:Echi no Takutsu|Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi]] - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * [[:en:Gwisil Boksin|Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto]] * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * [[:en:Liu Rengui|Liu Ren Gui]] * [[:en:Munmu of Silla|King Munmu]] * [[:en:Kim Yu-sin|Kim Yu-sin]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 07:39, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q55407023]] Wikidata name: Kosenokamusaki no Osa [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 04:18, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|en|Kanzaki District, Shiga|WD=Q8192952|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Gwisil Jipsa|en|Gwisil Jipsa|WD=Q845553|lt=Kishitsu no Jushi}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Matadera no Ōfuchi|ja|間人大蓋|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matadera no Ōfuchi|1=Hashihito no Ofuta|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407008}} - A general who survived the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Kawabe no Momoe|ja|河辺百枝|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kawabe no Momoe|1=kawabe no momoe|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406963}} - Another surviving general from the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Miwa no Nemaro|ja|三輪根麻呂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miwa no Nemaro|1=Miwa no Kimi Nemaro|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407015}} - A fellow middle general in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Azumi no Hirafu|ja|安曇比羅夫|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Azumi no Hirafu|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 阿曇比羅夫|1=Azumi no Hirafu|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q10946881}} - A general who perished in the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Echi no Takutsu|en|Echi no Takutsu|WD=Q11521589|lt=Pak Ichi Shintaraitsukeshi}} - Another fallen general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Saihi Pinang|ja|狭井檳榔|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saihi Pinang|1=sai no ajimasa|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55406942}} - A general at the Battle of Baekgang. * {{ill|Rokuhara no Omi|ja|廬原臣|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokuhara no Omi|1=Rokuhara no Omi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q55407030}} - Another general at Baekgang. * {{ill|Gwisil Boksin|en|Gwisil Boksin|WD=Q711668|lt=Kishitsu no Fuku no Makoto}} * {{ill|Suruga Castle|ja|周留城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Suruga Castle|1=Suruga Castle|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11416926}} * {{ill|Liu Rengui|en|Liu Rengui|WD=Q701534|lt=Liu Ren Gui}} * {{ill|Munmu of Silla|en|Munmu of Silla|WD=Q494532|lt=King Munmu}} * {{ill|Kim Yu-sin|en|Kim Yu-sin|WD=Q494501|lt=Kim Yu-sin}}

'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|en|Kanzaki District, Shiga|WD=Q8192952|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Gwisil Jipsa|en|Gwisil Jipsa|WD=Q845553|lt=Kishitsu no Jushi}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.

Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.

His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|en|Kanzaki District, Shiga|WD=Q8192952|lt=Kanzaki District}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Gwisil Jipsa|en|Gwisil Jipsa|WD=Q845553|lt=Kishitsu no Jushi}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu}}. revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:巨勢神前訳語]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097|1=Male (gender)}}}}
'''Kose no Kamusaki no Osamu''' (巨勢神前 訳語, dates of birth and death unknown) was a [[:en:Japanese nobility|nobleman]] and military figure of the {{ill|Asuka period|en|Asuka period|WD=Q210688|lt=Asuka period|1=Asuka period}}. His {{ill|Kabane|en|Kabane|WD=Q1664099|lt=Kabane|1=Kabane}} title was {{ill|Omi (title)|en|Omi (title)|WD=Q2574718|lt=Omi|1=Omi (title)}}.
== Lineage == The Kose no Kamusaki clan (巨勢神前臣) was a branch of the {{ill|Kose clan|ja|巨勢氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kose clan|qq=}} that resided in {{ill|Kanzaki District, Shiga|en|Kanzaki District, Shiga|WD=Q8192952|lt=Kanzaki District|1=Kanzaki District, Shiga}}, {{ill|Ōmi Province|en|Ōmi Province|WD=Q843882|lt=Ōmi Province|1=Ōmi Province}}. In the 4th year of Emperor Tenji's reign (665), it was recorded that: {{quotation|Again, over 400 men and women of Baekje were relocated to Kamizaki District in Ōmi Province.<ref name="#1">''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 4th Year, 2nd Month entry.</ref>}} This area became a refuge for Baekje nobles like {{ill|Gwisil Jipsa|en|Gwisil Jipsa|WD=Q845553|lt=Kishitsu no Jushi|1=Gwisil Jipsa}}, who fled to Japan after Baekje’s fall. Another record states: {{quotation|In the same month (March), rice fields were granted to the people of Baekje in Kamizaki District.<ref name="#1"/>}}
== Career == In the 2nd year of Emperor Tenji’s reign (663), Osamu led troops as a middle general during the campaign against {{ill|Silla|en|Silla|WD=Q28456|lt=Silla|1=Silla}}, serving under the senior general {{ill|Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|ja|上毛野稚子|1=Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wani no Wakako|13=User:Immanuelle/Kamitsukeno no Wakatoko|qq=}} and rear general {{ill|Abe no Hirafu|en|Abe no Hirafu|WD=Q479775|lt=Abe no Hirafu|1=Abe no Hirafu}}. He reportedly commanded an army of 27,000 soldiers.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 3rd Month entry.</ref> His involvement in the {{ill|Battle of Baekgang|en|Battle of Baekgang|WD=Q608727|lt=Battle of Baekgang|1=Battle of Baekgang}} is mentioned,<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 8th Month, 27th and 28th Day entries.</ref> but whether he survived and returned to Japan remains unclear.<ref>''Nihon Shoki,'' Emperor Tenji, 2nd Year, 9th Month entries (7th, 11th, 13th, 24th, and 25th Days).</ref>
== Notes == {{Footnotes}} {{reflist|2}}
== References == * ''Nihon Shoki'' (Volumes V), [[Iwanami Bunko]], 1994–1995. * ''Nihon Shoki: Full Modern Translation (Part II),'' [[Kodansha Gakujutsu Bunko]], Translated by [[Ujitani Takeshi]], 1988. * ''The Formation of Ancient Japan,'' [[Naoki Kōjirō]], Chūō Kōronsha, 1965. * ''Encyclopedia of Ancient Japanese Clans'' (Revised Edition), [[Saeki Ariyoshi]], Yūzankaku, 2015.
